skill.name: database-migration-helper
skill.description: Creates database migration files following project conventions for Prisma, Sequelize, Alembic, Knex, TypeORM, and other ORMs. Use when adding tables, modifying schemas, or when user mentions database changes.
skill.summary: Generate and review database migrations for common ORM frameworks.
skill.author: CrazyDubya

security_audit.summary: Static command and crypto alerts were reviewed in context. The crypto and reconnaissance hits are false positives from markdown examples and migration template text, but the skill intentionally permits Bash and recommends ORM commands that can modify databases.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Capable Migration Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access and tells the assistant to generate or run ORM migration commands. These commands are legitimate for the skill, but they can modify local databases and should require user confirmation before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly allows Bash and lists migration generation commands for several frameworks. The context is legitimate database migration work, so the concern is operational impact rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Potentially Destructive Database Operations
medium_findings.description: The reference material includes rollback and reset commands and templates include drop-table rollback examples. These are expected migration patterns, but misuse can remove data or reset schemas.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive operations are present as documented migration and rollback examples. They are not hidden or malicious, but they can cause data loss if run against the wrong database.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-crypto alerts map to migration wording such as hash index types, timestamp formats, and revision identifiers. No evidence found of cryptographic functions or weak hashing APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation and database terminology, not cryptographic implementation. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, or password hashing code.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Alerts Are Type Annotation False Positives
low_findings.description: The JavaScript template import detections are JSDoc or TypeScript type references for ORM migration APIs. They do not dynamically load attacker-controlled modules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import strings are inside comments used for editor typing. No executable dynamic import expression or user-controlled module path was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Database Tool Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs use of framework CLIs such as Prisma, Sequelize, Knex, TypeORM, Alembic, Django, and Rails. These invocations can create files or alter schemas depending on the project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and tied to the skill purpose. The pattern is dangerous only if executed without confirming target environment and command arguments.

content.value_statement: Database schema changes often need framework-specific migration files and safe rollback plans. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create migrations that match project conventions.
actual_capabilities: Detects common migration frameworks from project files.
Reviews existing migration naming and directory conventions.
Creates migration files for Prisma, Sequelize, Knex, TypeORM, Alembic, Django, and Rails.
Includes up and down migration guidance when the framework supports rollback.
Suggests checks for data loss, locks, indexes, and staging validation.
limitations: It cannot guarantee a migration is safe for production data.
It needs access to existing project files to match local conventions.
It may use Bash commands that should be approved before execution.
It does not replace database administrator review for high-risk schema changes.

use_cases.title: Add a New Application Table
use_cases.description: Create a migration for a new table with primary keys, timestamps, indexes, and rollback support.

use_cases.title: Modify an Existing Schema
use_cases.description: Add columns, constraints, indexes, or foreign keys while following project migration patterns.

use_cases.title: Review Production Migration Risk
use_cases.description: Check a planned migration for reversibility, locking risk, data loss, and staging test needs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new table or column.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration to add [table or column]. Follow the conventions in this project and explain any rollback step.

prompt_templates.title: Match Existing Conventions
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a repository already has migration history.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the existing migrations, identify the framework and naming pattern, then create a migration for [schema change].

prompt_templates.title: Add Indexes and Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for performance or relational integrity changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration that adds [index or constraint] to [table]. Include rollback logic and note possible locking risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Risky Production Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for data-sensitive or large-table changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe migration plan for [production schema change]. Include phased rollout steps, rollback limits, and staging validation.

output_examples.input: Add a nullable phone field to users in a Rails project.
output_examples.output: A Rails migration file name, a reversible migration plan, and a short note to test rollback in development.

output_examples.input: Create a Prisma migration for a products table.
output_examples.output: A Prisma migration directory suggestion, table fields, indexes, and a reminder to review generated SQL before deployment.

output_examples.input: Review this migration for production risk.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering data loss, locks, reversibility, dependent code, backups, and staging tests.

best_practices: Review generated SQL before applying it to shared environments.
Run migrations and rollbacks on development or staging data first.
Keep schema changes small and focused on one purpose.
anti_patterns: Running reset or rollback commands against production without explicit approval.
Combining unrelated schema and data changes in one migration.
Dropping columns before dependent application code is safely deployed.

faq.question: Which migration tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for Prisma, Sequelize, Knex, TypeORM, Alembic, Django, Rails, Flyway, and Liquibase.

faq.question: Can it run migration commands automatically?
faq.answer: It can suggest or run commands when Bash is available, but database-changing commands should require user approval.

faq.question: Does it create rollback logic?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for down, downgrade, rollback, or reversible migration logic when the framework supports it.

faq.question: Can it detect my project framework?
faq.answer: Yes. It looks for common files such as Prisma schemas, knexfile.js, alembic.ini, and Rails migration directories.

faq.question: Is it safe for production migrations?
faq.answer: It helps identify risks, but production migrations still need backups, staging tests, and human review.

faq.question: Does it handle data migrations?
faq.answer: It provides guidance for data migrations, including batching and rollback warnings for large or destructive changes.
